behavior: In AutoHide coroutine, on the Epilogue Stage field calls DialogManager.ForceControlByEvent(false) and yield-breaks (skips the timed auto-close wait), handing control back to the event.
notes: Real fldMapNo == gate (field 3009 = Epilogue: Stage). A custom-id fork of 3009 falls through to the normal timed AutoHide instead of returning control to the event script -> the scripted epilogue cutscene's dialog handshake breaks (control not yielded as the .eb expects), can hang/desync the epilogue. The epilogue is heavily scripted so impact is high.

behavior: Same as 3009: ForceControlByEvent(false) + yield-break in AutoHide for the Epilogue Stage field 3010.
notes: Real fldMapNo == gate (field 3010 = Epilogue: Stage). Fork loses the event-control handoff in AutoHide; same break as 3009.

behavior: In Close(), on Alexandria/Main Street when Map-byte-31 var == 3 (Puck hits Vivi), immediately calls dialog.AfterHidden() to force-finish the dialog instead of waiting for the close animation.
notes: Real fldMapNo == gate (field 100 = Alexandria/Main Street, intro). A custom-id fork loses the immediate AfterHidden() flush during the Puck-hits-Vivi scripted beat, so the dialog's AfterHidden side-effects (ProcessDialog/release) don't fire on the scripted close -> the intro cutscene handshake can hang. Also gated on map-var 31 == 3 (story state). High-value gate for the intro/opening fork work.

behavior: Hotfix: clears the whole party (sets 4 slots to NONE) right after obtaining the Gulug Stone, even if AllCharactersAvailable=2.
notes: Palace/Dungeon. This is a script-correctness hotfix; the comment notes the script normally adds characters back. A fork at a custom id misses it, so with AllCharactersAvailable=2 the party isn't cleared and the downstream re-add logic can desync the cast — potential soft-lock / wrong party for the cutscene. Scenario+entrance gated too.

behavior: Hotfix: forces the party to be exactly Oeilvert's team (Zidane + up to 3 of Garnet/Amarant/Quina/Freya/Vivi/Steiner/Eiko chosen by VARL_GenBool_3536..3542) when bringing the Gulug Stone back.
notes: Palace/Hall. Comment says it prevents another soft-lock later. A fork loses the forced-team correction => possible soft-lock / wrong party in the following cutscene. Scenario gated.

behavior: Hotfix: ensures at least one normal character (forces Zidane+Steiner) for the Esto Gaza Priest cutscene if the party has none of Garnet/Steiner/Freya/Quina/Amarant.
notes: Esto Gaza/Altar. Guards a cutscene that needs a normal actor present. A fork loses it => the cutscene can run with an unsupported party and soft-lock. Scenario gated.

behavior: Hotfix: forces a normal team (Zidane/Vivi/Steiner/Garnet) for the cutscene at the bottom of Gulug when Zidane or Vivi is missing or Eiko/non-standard members are present.
notes: Gulug/Path. Cutscene party-shape guard. A fork loses it => possible soft-lock with wrong party. Scenario+flag gated.

behavior: After the Beatrix battle in Burmecia/Palace, repositions and re-poses 7 actors (Brahne, Kuja, Zidane, Vivi, Freya, Quina, Beatrix) to fixed coordinates/animations (fix #664).
notes: Burmecia/Palace post-battle reinit. A fork at a custom id loses the actor repositioning => the after-battle cutscene actors are in wrong spots/poses, and the scripted scene can break/soft-lock. Also gated on battleMapIndex==4. UID-keyed actor fixes (11/13/15/16/19/20/23).

behavior: UpdateActiveTri: early-returns to EXEMPT the actor from per-frame walkmesh tri-snapping
notes: THE canonical case. 2504 sid14 = I.Castle/Small Room Chest_TerranA (Fork chest); 105 sid4 = Alexandria/Alley Dante (ladder-climbing signmaker); 2605 sid11 = Terra/Treetop Chest_TerranA (Remedy chest). These actors live off the walkmesh (on a ladder / a chest floating above floor). Without the exemption UpdateActiveTri calls ResetPos every frame -> actor reset/cutscene softlock (the proven Dante case). Highest-priority gate for the fork->donor-id remap patch.

behavior: Starts DelayedActiveTri coroutine that disables NPC char-collision and specific walkmesh triangles after 0.5s (ladders+stairs room)
notes: Also gated again at line 139 inside the coroutine. Disables tris 174/175/177/178 and de-activates non-player actor collision. A fork loses both → the ladder/stairs walkmesh isn't carved → player/NPCs can walk into blocked geometry or a scripted climb cutscene mis-navigates; high softlock risk on this navigation-heavy room. Engine remap should cover 2507.

gate: fldMapNo == 1656 && playerController == null (Iifa Tree/Eidolon Mountain) in EBG_scene2DScrollRelease
behavior: Binds the player controller to obj uid 8 (selects Zidane) when none is set, before a scroll-release
notes: Also see 1972 (SceneService3DScroll CrutchForEvaMap on 1656). This 'crutch' picks the controllable actor for a scripted scroll where playerController is null. A fork loses it → playerController stays null → the scroll-release / camera-follow has no player to track during this Eidolon cutscene → camera/scroll can hang or the beat doesn't advance. High softlock risk; engine remap should cover 1656.

gate: map == 2512 && playerController == null (CrutchForIpsenMap, EVT_IPSEN_IP_CNT_2) in SceneService3DScroll
behavior: Binds player controller to obj uid 2 when null, so 3D scroll can track the player
notes: Same 'crutch' pattern as 1656. Fork loses → playerController null during this Ipsen cutscene scroll → camera/scroll has nothing to follow → beat can stall. Softlock risk; engine remap should cover 2512.

behavior: Chocobo's Paradise: sets the Gold Chocobo actor's turn rate (omega) to 96 instead of the default 16.
notes: Actor ctor; gated on actor sid==4. A fork gets the default omega=16 -> the gold chocobo turns/orients slower. Playable, animation/movement quirk.

behavior: Iifa Tree/Eidolon Mound: sets Vivi actor's turn rate (omega) to 48 instead of default 16.
notes: Actor ctor; sid==9. Fork gets default omega -> Vivi turns slower in that scene. Movement quirk, playable.

behavior: Selects the combined-mesh background build path (CreateSceneCombined) instead of per-sprite CreateScene, for fields with depth-sorting/transparency problems
notes: id-keyed List<Int16> of fldMapNo (note: keyed on the numeric id, NOT the FBG name, so a fork DOES lose it). Fork → combineMeshes=false → uses the non-combined render path → these specific fields can show overlay depth-sort/transparency artifacts (e.g. Crystal World, Iifa roots, Oeilvert). Visual correctness regression but playable.

If reached, a fork loses the overlay-17 special-case → depth artifacts on that street overlay. Note the body 'i==17 ? true : true' is a no-op anyway.

notes: Same 552 gate inside the combine path. Fork loses the separate-sprite carve-out → those 4 sprites depth-sort wrong if the combine path is taken. Playable cosmetic-ish quirk.

Playable but a minigame input quirk. (Chocobo H&C is known to be field-id gated; the project notes recommend forking in-place on 2950-2952.)

notes: Real fldMapNo == gate (field 2950 = Chocobo's Forest). A custom-id fork loses the dig-confirm suppression. Same family as line 733.

notes: Real fldMapNo == gate (field 3011 = Epilogue: Stage). A fork loses the ForceControlByEvent(true) + the language-dependent wait fudge, so the epilogue dialog auto-hide timing/control is off (text may auto-advance with wrong timing). Less severe than 3009/3010 since it doesn't yield-break, but still an epilogue cutscene desync.

notes: Field 2801 = FBG_N55_DGLO_MAP777_DG_SRH_0 (Daguerreo Right Hall, Gilgamesh). Gated additionally on FF9StateSystem.Settings.IsFastTrophyMode (an optional speedrun/trophy assist), sid 11, ip 3834. A fork loses the trophy-mode skip only; normal play unaffected.

notes: Field 1900 = FBG_N20_TRNO_MAP417_TR_BAR_0 (Treno Pub, second instance). Gated on IsFastTrophyMode (optional), sid 0, ip 4138. Lost only in trophy mode; normal play unaffected.

behavior: In the expression evaluator, when this exact NPC/IP is hit on Gizamaluke Bell Room, advances s1.ip by 7 and returns 0 early -- skips an expression (a bugfix/patch for the Female Moogle).
notes: Field 705 = FBG_N12_GZML_MAP254A_GZ_BEL_0 (Gizamaluke Bell Room, Female Moogle). NOT inside IsFastTrophyMode -- this runs every playthrough. A fork at a custom id loses this expr skip; effect is whatever bug the patch fixes for that Moogle's script (playable, likely a soft/cosmetic glitch). sid 3, ip 541.

behavior: Widescreen fix: in a less-than comparison, rewrites the literal 80 to 300 so the Treno gate trigger position is correct in widescreen.
notes: Fields 908 = FBG_N20_TRNO_MAP400_TR_GAT_0 and 1908 = the second Treno gate instance. Comment: 'fix for gates at treno in widescreen'. notes: Field 657 = FBG_N15_KUIN_MAP317_KM_SWP_1 (Qu's Marsh / Pond, frog catching). Runs every playthrough (not trophy-gated). sid 17 (Zidane). A fork loses the inclusive-boundary fix for frog catching at those 13 IPs -- minigame edge-case behavior changes; playable.

notes: Field 2919 = FBG_N45_CYSW_MAPX19_CW_EMP_0 (Crystal World, where Excalibur II is grabbed). Only active when the optional Configuration.Hacks.ExcaliburIINoTimeLimit toggle is on; a custom-id fork loses the no-time-limit assist (timer reads real value). Vanilla behavior (timed) is preserved, so this is a lost QoL hack, not a softlock. Not field-id-pinned to vanilla logic.

behavior: On the Chocobo Hot & Cold maps, when a child actor's parent (Choco) changes idle/walk/run state, force-SetAnim the child to the matching clip so it visibly re-animates; off these maps the child only copies the anim id without calling SetAnim.
notes: Chocobo Hot & Cold fields. 'flag' is true only on the real ids; a fork at a custom id sets flag=false, so a parented actor's animation clip isn't refreshed on parent state change (anim id is still copied, but SetAnim/frame reset is skipped) -> child actor animation glitch/stale clip. Visible-but-playable. Note id 2953 appears in this OR but not in the line-73 sid table. Matches memory: Chocobo H&C is field-id gated (fork in-place on 2950-2952).

behavior: For one specific parented actor per Chocobo H&C map (the named sid), drive its animFrame independently (increment + modulo frameN) instead of slaving animFrame to the parent's animFrame.
notes: Chocobo Hot & Cold. Each clause pins a real field id AND a specific actor sid. A custom-id fork takes the else branch (animFrame = parent.animFrame), so this special actor (e.g. Choco/Mene) animates locked to its parent instead of free-running -> wrong/desynced animation, but playable. Note: 2953 has no sid clause here even though it's in the line-48 OR; this gate covers 2950/2951/2952/2954/2955.

behavior: When the No-Encounter setting is on, clears GlobBool 205/206/207 on Ice Cavern/Icicle Field & Ice Path to prevent the Wyerd forced battles.
notes: Only active when the IsNoEncounter setting is on; a fork at a custom id won't get the Wyerd-flag clearing, so the Wyerd battle would still trigger despite no-encounter. Wrong-but-playable, and conditional on a cheat/setting.

behavior: No-Encounter setting: clears GlobBool 238/239 on Ice Cavern/Ice Path.

notes: Conditional on IsNoEncounter. Fork loses the progression-flag set; a fork could get stuck on the forced sequence under no-encounter.

behavior: On Evil Forest/Nest, gates event-code processing on the dialog being fully visible (canProcessCode = !DialogManager.Activate || CompletlyVisible) to sync a scripted moment with dialog reveal.
notes: A fork at a custom id always runs code unconditionally (canProcessCode stays true) => the dialog/script timing sync on field 257 is lost. Wrong-but-playable timing/pacing quirk; could mis-time a scripted dialog beat.

behavior: Keeps two specific actors (sid 6 & 13) visible on field 52 while a flag (var 6357) is set, instead of disabling their renderers.
notes: Renderer-enable exception. The condition is built so on field 52 with sid 6/13 and var6357!=0 the disable is SKIPPED (actors stay shown). A fork at a custom id makes 'fldMapNo != 52' true => the exception fires for ALL actors and these two are disabled like normal => those actors get hidden when they shouldn't be. Visual/logic quirk on a fork (actors vanish). FUNCTIONAL-leaning visual.

behavior: During a specific scripted turn (turnAdd==32766) for model 273 at scenario 9370, wrap the candidate turn angle 'a' into [-180,180] before the completion comparison, normalizing the rotation so the turn terminates correctly.
notes: Field 2855 gated AND scenario-counter-gated (9370) AND model-gated (273). A fork loses the angle-normalization for this scripted turn; without it the completion test (a < turnRot) can mis-evaluate, leaving the actor turning the long way / overshooting. Cutscene-specific; wrong-but-recoverable rotation rather than a hard hang. A fork at scenario-zero also wouldn't hit SC 9370, so practically NA unless the fork seeds that beat -- but if seeded, the field-id pin still drops it.

behavior: Mirror of line 18 for the opposite turn direction (turnAdd==32767): wrap angle 'a' into [-180,180] before the completion comparison so the scripted turn terminates correctly.
notes: Same field 2855 + scenario 9370 + model 273 gate as line 18, the turnAdd==Int16.MaxValue path. Fork loses angle normalization on this scripted turn -> potential overshoot/long-way rotation. Same NA-unless-seeded caveat: requires SC 9370 to even reach, but if seeded the field-id pin drops it on a custom id.

behavior: Deactivates walkmesh triangle 56 (BGI_triSetActive(56,0)) for actor sid 4 on this field -- script-driven removal of a walkable tri (e.g. closing off a path/blocking a tri).
notes: turnOffTriManually is called from script (TurnOffTriManually opcode) to toggle a specific walkmesh tri by field. A custom-id fork of 1900 never matches, so the tri stays active -> the player can walk where the original blocks them (or a path that should open/close doesn't), a walkmesh logic quirk. Could in principle strand a scripted sequence but generally wrong-but-playable. sid 4 pinned.

gate: fldMapNo == 900 (with sid 8 and sid 17 sub-cases, scenario/map-index gated)
behavior: On field 900: sid 8 deactivates tri 56 once SC_COUNTER>=4450; sid 17 deactivates tri 62 unless SC>=4450 or map-index is 1/5 -- scenario-progress-driven walkmesh tri toggling.
notes: Field 900 pinned; inner branches further gated on SC_COUNTER_SVR (4450) and MAP_INDEX_SVR (1/5). A fork at a custom id never enters this block, so these progression-based walkmesh changes never apply -> tris in the wrong active state for the story beat (path open when it should be closed or vice versa). Wrong-but-playable; sids 8 and 17.

behavior: Deactivates walkmesh triangle 16 (BGI_triSetActive(16,0)) for actor sid 5 on this field.
notes: Field 1455 pinned + sid 5. A custom-id fork loses the scripted tri-16 deactivation, leaving that tri walkable when the original removes it -> walkmesh quirk, wrong-but-playable.

behavior: For sid 20 on field 2803, sets tris 105 and 106 active or inactive based on whether script var 761060 == 1 -- toggles a pair of walkmesh tris by a story flag.
notes: Field 2803 pinned + sid 20; tri activation driven by var 761060. A fork never runs this, so tris 105/106 keep their default active state regardless of the flag -> walkmesh region wrong (open/closed mismatch), wrong-but-playable. Could block/allow a route the script intends to gate.

behavior: id-keyed TABLE mapping a field's quad/trigger (composite key field*1000+objN) to a partner NPC uid; consumed by EventCollision.IsQuadTalkable (gMode==1 field mode) to make a tread-quad act as a talkable proxy for that NPC (directional talk fixups for card-game / interaction quads).
notes: TABLE QuadTalkableData (EventEngineUtils.cs). Keys decode field_id = key/1000: 111(Alexandria Town Beast St), 553,555,560,561,562 & 1303,1308 (Lindblum), 902,903,911,912 & 1860,1864,1902,1903,1911,1912 (Treno & ALXT_A reuse), 1509(Cleyra), 2103,2108,2109(Lindblum-W), 2303(Esto Gaza), 2455(Alexandria-W), 2802(Daguerreo). NOT extended by DictionaryPatch (unlike eventIDToFBGID/MESID), so a custom-id fork's composite key never matches -> the quad loses its talkable-proxy/directional behavior (an NPC the player should be able to talk to via a floor quad becomes non-interactive). Companion per-field fldMapNo branches (2103/2108/2109/2802) live in EventCollision.IsQuadTalkable (out of scope). Fork-remap engine patch must cover key/1000.

gate: QuadCircleData.ContainsKey(EMinigame.CreateNPCID(fldMapNo, quad.sid)) where key = fldMapNo*1000 + sid (in EventEngine.IsInQuadHotFix, gMode==1)
behavior: id-keyed TABLE replacing a field quad's collision shape with a circular (or augmented) region; IsInQuadHotFix uses it so the trigger/talk zone fires over a circle instead of the raw walkmesh quad (a collision hotfix for specific NPCs/triggers).
notes: TABLE QuadCircleData (EventEngineUtils.cs). Keys decode field_id = key/1000: 356(Dali Windmill), 404(Underground Dali), 561(Lindblum), 601(Lindblum CFR), 757(Burmecia), 916(Treno WHF), 1553(Cleyra Trunk camp), 2151(Lindblum Lift), 2222(Sanctuary), 2255(Ueno Underground vessel), 2901(Cleyra Sandwhirl), 2950 & 2952(Chocobo Hot&Cold). NOT extended by DictionaryPatch. A custom-id fork's composite key never matches -> the quad reverts to raw IsInQuad shape; the affected trigger/interaction may be unreachable or mis-shaped (e.g. Chocobo dig spots, lift call zone). Fork-remap engine patch must cover key/1000.

behavior: Forces the US-language .eb event binary to load for non-US/JP languages on Cleyra/Cathedral so the dancing scene uses the US/JP script variant (other-language binaries are broken for that scene).

A custom-id fork of 1060 in a non-US/JP language would load that language's own (broken) .eb for the dance instead of the US fallback -> the dancing scene could misbehave/desync. US/JP players and verbatim forks shipping the correct .eb unaffected. Edge case but a real field-id-pinned load redirect.

behavior: On fields 103 and 107, NPCs with sid 3 or 4 are exempted from being push-collided (CheckNPCPush returns false, so the player won't push/bump them).
notes: Equality-style gate (!=103 && !=107 short-circuits). A fork at a custom id falls through to the default result=true, so those NPCs become pushable -> the player can shove actors that vanilla pins in place. Movement/collision quirk, playable. Fields 103/107 are early Alexandria-castle area.

behavior: On field 1856, NPCs with sid 5 or 6 are exempted from push collision (not pushable by the player).
notes: A custom-id fork loses the exemption -> those two actors become pushable. Movement/collision quirk, not a softlock.

notes: Fork falls to default result=true -> the sid-6 quad is always pushable regardless of talkable state. Interaction quirk on the Treno card-game field; playable.

behavior: On field 2802, a quad with sid 24 is exempted from push collision.
notes: Fork loses the exemption -> the sid-24 quad becomes pushable. Collision quirk.

behavior: On field 2914, a quad with sid 13 is exempted from push collision.
notes: Fork loses the exemption -> sid-13 quad becomes pushable. Collision quirk.

behavior: On field 2504, a quad with sid 9 is made non-talkable.

behavior: Looks up a talkable-quad partner object in QuadTalkableData using a key built from (fldMapNo, quad.uid); if absent, IsQuadTalkable returns the default true.
notes: TABLE name = EventEngineUtils.QuadTalkableData, keyed via EMinigame.CreateNPCID(fldMapNo, uid). The known fldMapNo keys it is populated for (per the branches just below) are 2103, 2108, 2109, 2802. A custom-id fork uses fldMapNo=customId so the composite key never matches -> the talkable-quad pairing and per-field facing logic are all skipped, IsQuadTalkable falls through to 'return true'. Field-id-pinned. Playable interaction quirk.

behavior: On field 2108, a registered talkable quad is only talkable when the player faces within a specific direction window (dir 91-159), instead of the generic CollisionAngle test.
notes: Only reached when EventEngineUtils.QuadTalkableData contains key=CreateNPCID(fldMapNo,uid) - that key is itself field-id-derived, so a fork would not match the table either (see separate QuadTalkableData finding). On a fork the whole IsQuadTalkable special-casing collapses to the default 'return true' early-out, changing which direction the player must face to talk to the quad. Playable.

behavior: On field 2109, talkable-quad facing window is dir 160-222.
notes: Same QuadTalkableData precondition as 2108. Fork loses the per-field facing window. Playable interaction quirk.

behavior: On field 2103, talkable-quad facing window is dir 160-222.

behavior: HonoLateUpdate: when out of depth range, sets actor.frontCamera = (sid==16) for Zidane (Fossil Roo/Nest)

behavior: GeoAttach: special-cases PC curPos when attaching to a parent node within the Fossil Roo zone (1400-1425)

A custom-id fork (e.g. 6xxx) is OUTSIDE the 1400-1425 range, so the whole geo-attach reposition is skipped -> when the PC mounts the gargant the player isn't snapped onto it correctly. Functional (mount positioning) on Fossil Roo gargant fields; playable but visibly wrong. This is the one RANGE gate in these files.

behavior: In FMV service state 5, allows the field FMV to begin playing even when the PLAY attr bit was not set, specifically for field 100.

A verbatim fork that emits FF9FieldFMVPlay sets attr&1 and is unaffected; a fork relying on the implicit id==100 start would not begin the FMV. Not a softlock (service still completes), so FUNCTIONAL/COSMETIC at worst (missing intro movie).

behavior: Iifa Tree/Tree Trunk: force-closes all dialog boxes before the Mistodons battle swirl (a scripted-cutscene-to-battle hotfix).

behavior: MBG.UpdateCamera: applies the movie-background per-frame camera offset/RT to the field camera for ALL MBG fields EXCEPT 2752 (which keeps its own camera).

So the fork GAINS unwanted camera-driving on an MBG cutscene field -> wrong camera. MBG fields are special FMV-driven; forking them is exotic but the loss is functional.

behavior: MBG.UpdateCamera: Crystal World (2933) only -- repositions/clears flags on the actor (uid 4) along the scripted MBG path at specific frame numbers.

behavior: Crystal World (2933) only: drives MBGUpdate from Unity LateUpdate instead of HonoUpdate.
notes: Fork of 2933 loses the LateUpdate driver -> MBG runs on the wrong tick. Functional, exotic.

behavior: For field 2933, drives the streaming MovieMaterial.Update()/NativeUpdate() from Update() instead of LateUpdate().

A custom-id fork of 2933 falls into the LateUpdate branch (line 21) instead -> the in-field movie still updates but on the wrong phase, possibly a 1-frame lag / sync glitch. Movie still plays, so FUNCTIONAL (visual timing), not a hang.

behavior: Alexandria Castle/Library (3 versions): applies a z-depth offset of 700 to SPS_0008 (fixes draw-order/occlusion).

behavior: Palace/Hall: blocks opening the party-switch menu (plays the deny SFX and returns) when Zidane returns with the Gulug Stone -- prevents a soft-broken party state.

behavior: In AfterSoundFinished_Dismiss: for a timed dialog (EndMode>0) it returns early to let the timer close it, EXCEPT on Epilogue Stage 3009/3010 where it proceeds to force-close after the voice ends.

On real 3009/3010 the voice-end path force-closes a timed dialog; a custom-id fork of 3009/3010 is != 3009/3010 == TRUE for the exclusion -> takes the early-return branch -> the dialog is NOT force-closed on voice end and relies on the timer. Only matters with VoiceActing enabled. Epilogue cutscene auto-advance timing. Counterpart to Dialog.cs AutoHide 3009/3010.

behavior: In Prima Vista/Interior, defers the Add-mode fade by 3 frames (StartCoroutine SetGlobalFade) when fast-forwarding, so the fade renders correctly under FF.
notes: Only fires under FastForward setting; a fork at a custom id skips the deferred-fade tweak -> a 1-frame fade glitch at most during fast-forward. Cosmetic.

behavior: In Brahne's Fleet/Event at scenario 6930, defers the Add-mode fade by 3 frames so the global fade renders correctly.
